ps140: When the ice of ignorance beset all creation with hostile attack* and a multitude of idols were worshipped,* then, O glorious martyrs,* you put an end to it with zealous heart and the fervor of divine faith;* and you shed your blood for Him who shed His blood upon the Cross.

When, at God’s command,* you set out for supernatural struggles,* disregarding your perishable bodies,* then, strengthened by the power of the Almighty,* you were not afraid of fire or the cutting sword.* Therefore, bending your necks before God, O blessed ones,* you accepted death with joy.

The valiant passion-bearer Peon,* Valerian, Chariton and Charita,* divine Justin, Euelpistus, and the glorious Hierax:* they dyed their garments in divine blood* and were clothed in it together* now stand before Christ the King and Lord of all* with the angels in the heavens.
theotokion: We have truly acquired no other mighty refuge,* no pillar of strength, and no invincible rampart than You, O all-pure one.* To You we flee, and to You we cry out:* O Mistress, help us, lest we perish!* Show forth upon us Your grace, the glory of Your power,* and the greatness of Your loving-kindness.
stavrotheotokion: She who knew not wedlock,* beholding You nailed to the tree of the Cross,* O Jesus, said weeping:* “O my sweet Child, why have You forsaken me* who alone gave You birth,* O unapproachable Light of the unoriginate Father?* Hasten and glorify Yourself,* so that those who glorify Your divine sufferings* may receive divine glory!”
